evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: keeping domain outputs consistent, tested, and discoverable
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain the insights layer on top of governed domains, producing reusable KPI models, funnels, cohorts, and segmentations
evidence.jd_authorship: Model key business domains, including merchant activity, product adoption, lifecycle events, and risk scoring, building well-documented, quality-assured data products
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build and maintain the insights layer on top of governed domains, producing reusable KPI models, funnels, cohorts, and segmentations
evidence.autonomy_level: Partner with squads across the tribe on event design and data contracts, maintaining staging pipelines, applying modelling conventions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Model key business domains...building well-documented, quality-assured data products; Implement technical improvements including incremental processing strategies
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Building new quality-assured data products is paired with implementing technical improvements to existing processing strategies, reflecting both greenfield and fix/scale work.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tools, infrastructure, or GenAI responsibilities mentioned; standard analytics engineering and domain modeling work.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Candidate owns building and quality assurance of data products, with explicit ownership verb paired with quality expectation.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: keeping domain outputs consistent, tested, and discoverable
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Emphasis on operational consistency and discoverability suggests reliability and pipeline maintenance concerns rather than compliance/audit-primary framing.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals emphasizing quality, testing, documentation, and data governance with no velocity language.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: SumUp is fintech; JD explicitly mentions risk scoring, indicating regulatory and financial reporting implications.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Existing governance infrastructure, multiple established business domains, and reusable data products indicate a function beyond early stage that is scaling, not a mature multi-team operation.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical concepts (data contracts, modelling conventions, KPI models, funnels, cohorts) and concrete business examples show technical depth typical of hiring managers.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role's primary purpose is building data infrastructure and reusable analytics tools that serve other data consumers and analysts, not directly serving commercial, product, or finance stakeholders.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize partnership, maintenance, and application of established patterns, framing the role as executing on defined work rather than independently setting direction.
